q_test1.currentAnswer: No Revolut partnership with a specific Tel Aviv (Ben Gurion) airport lounge has been announced, so the situation is unchanged.  Revolut offers unlimited free access to over 1,600 airport lounges worldwide, and lets any customer book at those lounges regardless of plan.  This access is provided through third-party lounge networks rather than a Revolut-specific Tel Aviv lounge, and  Revolut is switching its airport lounge access partner from DragonPass to LoungeKey.  The only recent Ben Gurion lounge development is unrelated to Revolut:  American Express Israel and aviation company Jetex have partnered to open a new premium lounge at Ben Gurion Airport, with access restricted to holders of Israeli cards.
q_test1.currentSummary: Still no Revolut partnership with a Tel Aviv airport lounge

history.changeReason: The core answer remains that no Revolut Tel Aviv lounge partnership exists; the added details about lounge networks and an unrelated Amex/Jetex lounge do not change the underlying fact.

history.answer: No, I found no evidence of a Revolut partnership with any airport lounge in Tel Aviv. Revolut offers lounge access globally through third-party networks such as LoungeKey and DragonPass rather than through a specific Tel Aviv (Ben Gurion) airport lounge partnership.
history.summary: No Revolut–Tel Aviv airport lounge partnership found
